name: Zucchini Upside Down Corn Bread
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound Italian sausage (turkey) -- removed from casings
1 teaspoon finely chopped orange zest -- optional
recipeInstructions:
- Coarsely shred enough zucchini to make 1/3 cup; set aside. Cut the remainder into 1/4-inch slices.
- In a skillet over moderate heat, brown the sausage and crumble it well. Leave the drippings in the skillet, then remove the sausage to drain.
- Reduce the heat. Add the onion and zucchini slices to the pan. Cover and cook over medium-low heat until they are slightly tender, about 5 minutes, stirring gently twice.
- In a sprayed 8x1-1/2 inch round cake pan, starting in the center of the dish, arrange the cooked zucchini slices in concentric circles using all of the slices.
- Set the temperature to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
- Make the corn muffin mix according to package instructions for Johnnie Cake. Add the cheese, crumbled sausage, and shredded zucchini, and optional orange zest, to the cornbread batter. Spread the mixture evenly over the zucchini slices.
- Bake for 25 minutes (more or less) until the top is golden.
- Using a spatula, loosen the sides of the corn bread from the dish; invert it onto a serving platter. Let it stand a little, as needed. Serve warm. Great with honey-butter (mix equal parts together).
